Politics.headline: Furious Dems threaten Bondi impeachment, contempt after briefing
Politics.detail: Democratic members of the House Oversight Committee stormed out midway through a briefing from Attorney General Pam Bondi, enraged and raising the threat of an impeachment or contempt of Congress effort against her.

Politics.headline: One 'freaking snake' and no apologies: How the Mullin hearing went off the rails
Politics.detail: Oklahoma Sen. Markwayne Mullin faced his fellow lawmakers for a confirmation hearing to run the Department of Homeland Security and the hearing became heated and emotional at times as senators grilled Mullin on past comments and how he might differ from….

Politics.headline: Trump threatens to 'blow up' all of Iran's South Pars gasfield if Tehran strikes Qatar.
Politics.detail: Amid escalating tensions, Trump has threatened to destroy Iran's South Pars gasfield if Tehran attacks Qatar, signaling a potential widening of the conflict.

Politics.headline: US eases Venezuela oil sanctions as Trump seeks to boost world oil supply during Iran war
Politics.detail: In response to the ongoing Iran war, the U.S. is easing oil sanctions on Venezuela in an effort to increase the global oil supply.

Politics.headline: 'Unpleasant surprises': Will key French cities elect far-right mayors?
Politics.detail: France is preparing for potential shifts in its political landscape as key cities face the possibility of electing far-right mayors.

Finance & Economy.headline: IMF Staff Reaches Staff-level Agreement with Seychelles on Extended Fund Facility and Resilience and Sustainability Facility
Finance & Economy.detail: The IMF and Seychellois authorities have reached an agreement on policies to complete the final reviews of Seychelles' 36-month EFF and RSF arrangements, potentially disbursing $45 million upon approval. The Seychelles economy saw robust performance in 2025, with real GDP growth estimated at 5.1 percent, driven by record tourist arrivals, while consumer price inflation was just below zero.

Finance & Economy.headline: ASX 200 afternoon report: 19 March 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: Driven by Middle East conflicts and a hawkish Federal Reserve, the ASX 200 faces pressure, with materials stocks down and energy shares as rare gainers. The Australia 200 trades 140 points (-1.63%) lower at 8498 as of 3.20pm AEDT.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK to double steel tariffs to 50% to save plants from collapse
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK is set to double steel tariffs to 50% in an effort to protect domestic plants from collapse. This decision comes amidst ongoing challenges in the steel industry and aims to provide a safeguard against potential market instability.

Finance & Economy.headline: Labour Market Statistics - March 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: The number of employed people (age 16 and over) in Northern Ireland was estimated at 895,000, an increase of 15,000 from the last quarter but a decrease of 4,000 from the same period last year.  The latest NI seasonally adjusted unemployment rate for the period November-January 2026 was estimated at 2.2%.

Finance & Economy.headline: European Commission Concludes Merger Review
Finance & Economy.detail: The European Commission has concluded that a proposed transaction related to the physical and financial trading and distribution of precious metals would not raise competition concerns. The review found that the companies involved have a limited combined market position.

Technology.headline: Linux Foundation kicks off effort to shield FOSS maintainers from AI slop bug reports
Technology.detail: Big Tech donates $12.5 million to get things rolling to help shield maintainers from AI-generated bug reports.

Technology.headline: Japan to allow 'proactive cyber-defense' from October 1st
Technology.detail: Japan will allow proactive cyber-defense, also known as 'hacking back' or 'offensive cyber-ops' from October 1st.

Technology.headline: US startup advertises 'AI bully' role to test patience of leading chatbots
Technology.detail: A US startup is advertising an 'AI bully' role to test the patience of leading chatbots.

Technology.headline: Meta on trial over child safety: can it really protect its next generation of users?
Technology.detail: Meta is on trial over child safety, raising questions about its ability to protect its next generation of users.

Technology.headline: 2,400 Kaiser mental health professionals strike in Northern California over AI concerns
Technology.detail: Around 2,400 Kaiser mental health professionals are on strike in Northern California due to concerns over AI.

Science.headline: New subatomic particle discovered at CERN's Large Hadron Collider
Science.detail: A new subatomic particle known as the Ξcc⁺, a heavy proton-like particle containing two charm quarks, has been discovered at CERN's Large Hadron Collider using the upgraded LHCb experiment, confirming predictions and settling a decades-long question about its existence. Scientists observed it through its decay into lighter particles in high-energy collisions.

Science.headline: World Happiness Report 2026 shows heavy social media use linked to drop in wellbeing among young people
Science.detail: The World Happiness Report 2026 indicates that heavy social media usage is contributing to decreased wellbeing in young individuals, particularly girls, in English-speaking countries and Western Europe. The report, published by the Wellbeing Research Centre at the University of Oxford, also ranks Finland as the happiest country for the ninth consecutive year.

Science.headline: European Commission Adopts Work Programme for 2026-2027 Euratom Research and Training Programme
Science.detail: The European Commission has adopted a work program for the 2026 and 2027 Euratom Research and Training Programme, allocating €330 million to advance nuclear research, innovation, and training. This program aims to strengthen the EU's energy independence and support the transition to carbon neutrality by 2050.

Science.headline: New ice core studies expand histories of greenhouse gases and ocean temperature to 3 million years
Science.detail: New research using ice cores has expanded the historical record of greenhouse gases and ocean temperatures to 3 million years. This data provides insights into long-term climate patterns and helps scientists understand current climate change.

Science.headline: Scientists recreated a life-size oviraptor nest to understand how these dinosaurs hatched their eggs
Science.detail: Scientists recreated a life-size oviraptor nest to understand how these dinosaurs hatched their eggs. Their experiments showed the parent likely couldn't heat all the eggs directly, meaning sunlight played a key role.

Health.headline: Health profession regulators agree on education quality standards in Africa
Health.detail: Health professions regulatory bodies, education leaders and technical experts from across the African Region have reached a landmark consensus on the Africa Health Professions Education Quality Standards (AHPEQS), establishing a shared continental framework to strengthen the quality, relevance and responsiveness of health professional training. The agreement comes as the Region faces a projected shortage of 6.1 million health professionals by 2030.

Health.headline: The Pulse: Virginia Tech researcher develops experimental vaccine to combat opioid addiction; health career opportunities expand in Roanoke
Health.detail: Researchers at Virginia Tech are developing a vaccine that blocks opioids from reaching the brain, preventing their addictive effects. The experimental vaccine produced a strong immune response and improved protection against oxycodone in mice.

Health.headline: In debate over sugary drinks, Colorado weighs health benefits and personal choice
Health.detail: Colorado policymakers are weighing the health benefits and personal choices related to sugary drinks, considering a proposed “Healthy Choice” SNAP Waiver that would restrict the purchase of certain beverages with SNAP benefits. Advocacy groups raise concerns about potential confusion and food insecurity.

Health.headline: We're bringing families more healthy foods in a SNAP
Health.detail: U.S. Secretary of Agriculture Brooke L. Rollins and U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services Robert F. Kennedy Jr. published a joint opinion piece in Fox News highlighting the need to strengthen SNAP retailer stocking standards. They aim to modernize SNAP to promote healthy eating and empower Americans to lead better lives.

Health.headline: Meningitis: Cases rises to 20 in fatal Kent outbreak amid surging demand for private jabs.
Health.detail: A fatal outbreak of Meningitis in Kent has risen to 20 cases, causing a surge in demand for private vaccinations. Officials have confirmed that the outbreak is caused by a less targeted strain B.

Sports.headline: MLB teams pressured WBC managers to be careful with pitchers; Venezuela pushed back
Sports.detail: During the World Baseball Classic, MLB teams reportedly pressured managers to be cautious with pitchers, but Venezuela resisted these pressures.

Sports.headline: Tottenham latest: Tudor praises 'improved' Spurs after first win
Sports.detail: Igor Tudor praised Tottenham's improved performance following their first win.

Sports.headline: Barcelona Blitzes Newcastle to Reach Champions League Quarters
Sports.detail: Barcelona decisively defeated Newcastle to advance to the Champions League quarterfinals.

Sports.headline: Ovechkin scores his 922nd goal and Hutson scores in his debut as the Caps beat the Senators 4-1
Sports.detail: Alex Ovechkin scored his 922nd career goal, and Cole Hutson scored in his NHL debut as the Washington Capitals defeated the Ottawa Senators 4-1.

Sports.headline: Doncic drops 40, James adds 30 as Lakers stretch their streak to 7 with 124-116 win over Rockets
Sports.detail: Luka Doncic scored 40 points, LeBron James added 30, as the Los Angeles Lakers extended their winning streak to seven games with a 124-116 victory over the Houston Rockets.

Entertainment.headline: Joseph Duggar, Brother to Josh, Arrested for Alleged Unlawful Sexual Activity With 9-Year-Old Girl
Entertainment.detail: Joseph Duggar, brother of Josh Duggar, has been arrested for alleged unlawful sexual activity with a 9-year-old girl, according to Entertainment Tonight.

Entertainment.headline: Val Kilmer Resurrected by AI 1 Year After Death to Appear in New Film
Entertainment.detail: Val Kilmer, who passed away a year ago, has been brought back to the screen using AI technology for a new film, reports Entertainment Tonight.

Entertainment.headline: Ryan Gosling Is Down for a Harry Styles Musical Team Up After 'SNL' Cameos!
Entertainment.detail: Ryan Gosling has expressed his enthusiasm for a potential musical collaboration with Harry Styles following their respective appearances on 'Saturday Night Live'.

Entertainment.headline: 'Dhurandhar 2 The Revenge' Movie Review and Release Live Updates
Entertainment.detail: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has made it to the big screens today, on March 19, 2026 and the high expectations that the critics and audience had for the movie seem to have been met. Interestingly, the movie has outperformed its prequel.

Entertainment.headline: Oscars 2026 recap: 'One Battle After Another' wins best picture after big night
Entertainment.detail: Paul Thomas Anderson's “One Battle After Another” took home six Oscars, including best picture. Michael B. Jordan emerged victorious for his leading role in “Sinners,” while Jessie Buckley won best actress for “Hamnet”.

Crime & Justice.headline: Bahrain: Sweeping Arrests Amid Conflict
Crime & Justice.detail: Bahraini authorities have arrested dozens of people for exercising their right to peaceful expression, seeking the death penalty in some cases, amid the conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran. Authorities have arrested people for participating in peaceful demonstrations mourning the death of Ayatollah Khamenei or for protesting US and Israeli attacks in Iran.

Crime & Justice.headline: California used faulty DUI tests for nearly 10 years, state Justice Department says
Crime & Justice.detail: For nearly 10 years, the test kits used in California for DUI tests did not contain the necessary amount of the chemical compound sodium fluoride to prevent urine samples from fermenting, according to a letter from the state. Thousands of California prisoners falsely tested positive for opioids last year because of a laboratory mistake, and now hundreds of decisions to deny parole to some of those prisoners are getting a second look.

Crime & Justice.headline: Texas jails' failure to free inmates on time costing counties
Crime & Justice.detail: Reports indicate Dallas County has pointed to jail systems causing delays as early as 2005, with counties showing a “deliberate indifference” in ensuring timely release by pushing the blame to technological gaps. In the past two years, Dallas County commissioners have approved settlements in three lawsuits from inmates who accused the county of not releasing them from jail on time, two for $60,000 and one for $100,000.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Department Files Case to Revoke U.S. Citizenship of Mastermind Behind Multimillion-Dollar Tax Fraud Scheme.
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department announced that they are filing a case to revoke the U.S. Citizenship of the mastermind behind a multi-million dollar tax fraud scheme. News items published before January 20, 2025, can be found in the Archives.

Crime & Justice.headline: High court considers if officer names can be kept secret
Crime & Justice.detail: The South Dakota Supreme Court heard arguments regarding whether the names of two police officers involved in a shootout with a suspect in 2024 should be protected from public view under Marsy's Law. The high court’s ruling could make it possible for law enforcement officers to be considered "victims" of crimes and to have their names kept out of public documents.

Education.headline: AI Is Supercharging Cyberattacks. What Schools Need to Know and Do
Education.detail: Many school districts are ill-prepared to defend themselves against AI-powered cyberattacks, and some experts and district tech leaders are unsure what role the tech should play in cybersecurity.

Education.headline: Educators Sound the Alarm on the Dangers of Child Marriage
Education.detail: The U.S. has pledged to outlaw child marriage by 2030, but that plan is not on track, leaving thousands of students at risk each year, say educators.

Education.headline: NEA Files Emergency Motion to Stop ICE Enforcement Near Schools
Education.detail: The NEA filed an emergency motion to stop ICE enforcement near schools, citing the chaos and fear caused by federal agents around schools, churches, and other sensitive locations.

Education.headline: Three-quarters of nine-month-olds in England have 'daily screen time'
Education.detail: A recent study shows that the majority of nine-month-old babies in England are exposed to screens on a daily basis.

Education.headline: Shaquille O'Neal is offering to pay for the funeral of a 12-year-old Georgia girl who died days after she collapsed following a fight after school.
Education.detail: Shaquille O'Neal is offering financial assistance to the family of a 12-year-old girl in Georgia who passed away after collapsing in a fight after school.

Lifestyle.headline: Bars turn to 'tiny' cocktails as Americans trim drinking, spending amid rising prices
Lifestyle.detail: With rising prices, bars are offering smaller cocktails to cater to customers who are cutting back on spending and alcohol consumption.

Lifestyle.headline: Aldi recalls popular snack food over possible rodent hair contamination
Lifestyle.detail: Aldi has issued a recall for a popular snack food item due to the potential presence of rodent hair.

Lifestyle.headline: I let AI guide me through London for a day. Why do I keep being sent underground?
Lifestyle.detail: A writer explores London with the help of AI, encountering unexpected and sometimes frustrating results.

Lifestyle.headline: Wendy's launches March Madness menu with chance to win $100,000
Lifestyle.detail: Wendy's has introduced a special menu for March Madness, offering customers the chance to win $100,000.

Lifestyle.headline: Your daily horoscope Thursday March 19, 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Find out what the stars have in store for you with today's daily horoscope.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Before any Cuba deal, make Havana release the prisoners
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The U.S. should demand the unconditional release of all Cuban political prisoners before offering any sanctions relief or diplomatic thaw, as a government that seeks dollars while jailing dissidents is not reforming, it is refinancing repression. Conditioning engagement on liberty is a staple of American statecraft.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Now is time to strike on the Lower Hill District
Opinion/Editorial.detail: After two decades, the costs of delay are apparent, and so there appears to be momentum toward achieving a workable solution for the Lower Hill District in Pittsburgh. The land is finally in more capable hands, after the Penguins hockey club relinquished its development rights last October.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Europe can't stand Trump. It also can't live without him.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: “Derisking” the continent from the U.S. will lead in only one direction: dependence on China. Some European leaders have sought to use the moment of the Iran war to distance themselves from Washington, expressing a desire to reduce their dependence on U.S. tech and defense.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: LFP Letters to the Editor: March 19, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Nunavut MP Lori Idlout has left the NDP for the governing Liberals, betraying the party and those who worked for her election. In accepting her and other floor-crossers, Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberals have underlined they will do just about anything to cling to power.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Morning Briefing: March 19, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Qatar declared the military and security attaches at the Iranian Embassy in Doha “persona non grata” and asked them to leave the country within 24 hours, in light of repeated Iranian targeting and brutal aggression on Qatar's sovereignty and security. The US Federal Reserve kept its benchmark rate unchanged in an 11-1 vote in the 3.5-3.75% range.

World.headline: War escalates, energy prices spike after Israeli strike on Iran gas field
World.detail: As Iran retaliates for an Israeli strike on the South Pars gas field, one analyst warns the war is "now hitting the plumbing of the global energy system."

World.headline: UAE says seven missiles, 15 drones intercepted from Iran today
World.detail: The United Arab Emirates reported intercepting seven missiles and 15 drones from Iran today amidst escalating tensions in the region.

World.headline: EU leaders slam Hungary's Orban for blocking Ukraine aid package
World.detail: European Union leaders have criticized Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban for obstructing the approval of an aid package for Ukraine.

World.headline: Oil prices soar as Iran targets energy facilities across Persian Gulf
World.detail: The price of Brent crude climbed past $115 per barrel as Qatar, Saudi Arabia and Kuwait reported more attacks on infrastructure and the U.S. and Tehran traded threats.

World.headline: Trump threatens to 'blow up' huge Iranian gas field if Iran attacks Qatar
World.detail: Amidst escalating tensions, President Trump has threatened to destroy a vast Iranian gas field if Tehran retaliates for an Israeli strike by attacking Qatar's infrastructure.

Zimbabwe.headline: Fuel prices surge in Zimbabwe
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has experienced a surge in fuel prices, adding to the economic challenges faced by citizens. The increase is the second one this month, driven by rising cost pressures.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mugabe son's lawyers, prosecution '98%' close to reaching plea deal
Zimbabwe.detail: Bellarmine Mugabe's lawyers and the prosecution are reportedly close to reaching a plea deal, with guilty pleas expected to lead to a reduced sentence. The case has been ongoing, and this development suggests a potential resolution.

Zimbabwe.headline: Ex-detective Muvevi denies 4 murder charges, claims goblin influence
Zimbabwe.detail: Former detective Jaison Muvevi, accused of multiple murders, has denied the charges, claiming he was influenced by evil spirits and goblins. His lawyer stated that Muvevi was being haunted by these entities at the time of the crimes.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt prioritises women's health in public institutions
Zimbabwe.detail: The government of Zimbabwe is prioritizing women's health within public institutions, signaling a focus on improving healthcare services for women. This initiative aims to address specific health needs and ensure better access to care.

Zimbabwe.headline: Retired generals warn Mnangagwa against changing constitution without referendum
Zimbabwe.detail: Retired generals have cautioned President Mnangagwa against amending the constitution without holding a referendum. This warning highlights concerns over proposed constitutional changes and the need for public input.
